Effects: Promotes the integrity of the intestinal mucus layer, maintains gut barrier function, and may reduce allergic reactions while supporting skin health.
Effects: Possesses antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, helps stabilize the gut microbiota, and improves intestinal barrier function.
Effects: Helps maintain an acidic environment in the gut, inhibits pathogenic bacteria, and may alleviate symptoms of lactose intolerance.
Effects: Enhances the intestinal barrier, regulates immune function, and helps prevent or improve intestinal issues such as diarrhea.
Effects: Produces antimicrobial substances, inhibits pathogenic bacteria, and may alleviate intestinal inflammation.
Effects: Promotes intestinal mucosal health, regulates immune function, and helps reduce the number of pathogenic bacteria in the gut.
Effects: Improves intestinal motility, relieves constipation, and helps maintain the balance of the gut microbiota.
Effects: Often associated with intestinal infections and inflammation, and excessive proliferation may have adverse effects on health.
Effects: Some strains are pathogenic bacteria and can easily cause diarrhea or infections.

Effects: Associated with intestinal inflammation. Excessive levels may indicate or exacerbate imbalances in the gut ecosystem.
Effects: Breaks down fibre to produce short-chain fatty acids, generally benefiting gut health.
Effects: A normal commensal bacterium, though some strains may produce toxins and cause intestinal infections.
Effects: Assists in breaking down polysaccharides and maintaining intestinal balance. It is considered a beneficial bacterium for gastrointestinal health.
Effects: A common intestinal commensal bacterium that helps maintain microbial stability and supports metabolic health.
Effects: Metabolises dietary fibre to produce beneficial acids, supporting intestinal barrier function and overall health.
Effects: Breaks down xylan to produce short-chain fatty acids, positively influencing the gut environment.
Effects: Breaks down fibre to produce substances such as butyrate, aiding in maintaining the health of intestinal epithelial cells.
Effects: An important producer of butyrate, with anti-inflammatory properties that help maintain intestinal mucosal integrity.
Effects: Activates intestinal receptors, strengthens tight junctions in intestinal epithelial cells, and reinforces the intestinal barrier.
Effects: One of the most common symbiotic bacteria, it helps alleviate ulcerative colitis inflammation.
Effects: Helps assess the risk of inflammatory bowel disease, improves metabolic abnormalities, and enhances intestinal barrier function.
Effects: Utilizes lactic acid to produce propionic acid, potentially exerting positive effects on metabolism and neurotransmission.
Effects: A highly efficient symbiotic bacterium thatbreaks down polysaccharides, aiding in nutrientabsorption and maintaining intestinal balance.
Effects: One of the primary butyrate-producing bacteria, with potent anti-inflammatory effects.
